recall.meaning: Out of ALL real fraud transactions, what % did the model catch? Most important metric for fraud detection — missing real fraud is dangerous. Target: > 0.85
recall.our_result: Model caught 731 out of 800 real fraud transactions (91.4%). Missed 69 fraud transactions.

precision.meaning: Out of ALL transactions flagged as fraud, what % were actually fraud? Low precision = too many false alarms = review team overwhelmed. Target: > 0.60
precision.our_result: Of 936 total fraud alerts raised, 731 were genuine and 205 were false alarms (78.1% hit rate).

roc_auc.formula: Area under ROC curve (True Positive Rate vs False Positive Rate)
roc_auc.meaning: Overall model quality — how well it distinguishes fraud from normal across ALL thresholds. 0.5 = random guessing, 1.0 = perfect. Target: > 0.90
roc_auc.our_result: Score of 0.9863 means excellent discriminating ability between fraud and normal transactions.

pr_auc.meaning: Better than ROC-AUC for imbalanced datasets like fraud detection. Measures model quality specifically on the minority class (fraud). Random classifier baseline = fraud rate (0.08). Target: > 0.70
pr_auc.our_result: Score of 0.9443 is significantly above the random baseline of 0.08 — model is highly effective on fraud class.

f1_score.meaning: Harmonic mean of precision and recall. Useful single number when you want balance between both. For fraud detection recall matters more, so we use it alongside F1 not instead of it.

accuracy.meaning: Overall % of correct predictions. MISLEADING for fraud detection — a model predicting everything as normal gets 92% accuracy but catches zero fraud. Not used as primary metric.

true_negatives.meaning: Normal transactions correctly identified as normal — no action needed

false_positives.meaning: Normal transactions incorrectly flagged as fraud — false alarms. Go to manual review. Annoying but not dangerous.

false_negatives.meaning: Real fraud transactions missed by the model — most dangerous outcome. These fraudulent transactions go undetected. Must minimize this.

true_positives.meaning: Real fraud transactions correctly caught — the best outcome

threshold_analysis.explanation: The model outputs a fraud probability (0.0 to 1.0) per transaction. The threshold decides at what probability we call it fraud. Lower = catch more fraud (higher recall) but more false alarms. Higher = fewer false alarms but miss more fraud. Recommended for banking: 0.5

feature_importance.explanation: How much each feature contributed to the model's decisions. Higher = more important. A healthy model has importance spread across multiple features — not dominated by one.

model_parameters.algorithm_explanation: An ensemble of decision trees where each tree learns from the mistakes of the previous one. Industry standard for tabular fraud detection.

n_estimators.meaning: Number of trees in the ensemble. More = better but slower.

max_depth.meaning: Max depth of each tree. Too deep = memorises training data. Too shallow = misses patterns. 6 is a good default.

learning_rate.meaning: How much each new tree corrects the previous ones. Lower = more careful learning, better generalization.

scale_pos_weight.meaning: Ratio of normal to fraud samples (11.5x). Tells XGBoost to weight fraud examples more heavily to compensate for class imbalance.

subsample.meaning: Use 80% of rows per tree — adds randomness, prevents overfitting.

colsample_bytree.meaning: Use 80% of features per tree — adds randomness, prevents overfitting.

descriptions.amount_log: Log-scaled transaction amount — compresses wide value range
descriptions.amount_vs_avg_ratio: Transaction amount / customer avg spend — detects unusual spend
descriptions.amount_zscore: Std deviations from customer's normal spend — personalised signal
descriptions.hour_of_day: Hour of transaction (0–23) — time pattern signal
descriptions.is_night: 1 if transaction between 11 PM and 4 AM, else 0
descriptions.is_foreign_int: 1 if transaction from a foreign location, else 0
descriptions.is_suspicious_merchant: 1 if merchant is CASINO / CRYPTO etc., else 0
descriptions.is_new_account: 1 if account age < 90 days — new accounts are higher risk
descriptions.txn_type_encoded: Transaction type as number (UPI=0, NEFT=1, IMPS=2 etc.)
descriptions.account_age_days: Age of customer account in days
descriptions.spend_std: Customer's spending std deviation — measures spend variability
